page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core execution, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both read and write operations on large blocks ranking in the top ten percent of tested hardware. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU scores falling in the average category.

While the server demonstrates strong performance in multi-threaded tasks and database operations, certain workloads show distinct limitations. For instance, LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model is rated as poor, though text generation for medium and large models reaches strong tiers. Given the 150 Gbps network baseline and high database benchmark scores, this instance is suited for network-intensive applications, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and database workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of memory, yielding a 1:4 core-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the m8gn.12xlarge delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations, alongside high memory bandwidth. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are weaker, landing in the average and poor categories respectively. This hardware profile is suited for network-heavy applications, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARMv9 Graviton-powered instance featuring 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARMv9 Graviton server with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring high network bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and strong multi-threaded database performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps
Achieves top-tier benchmarks in multi-core processing and database operations
Shows lower relative performance in single-core tasks and small-model LLM text generation
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and network-intensive workloads
